chunks.title: Comparison of Functions #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now, a combination of two functions. The functions may be time functions or they may be space

chunks.title: Comparison of Functions #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: functions. So, functions, mathematical functions. If suppose I have one function that is n square

chunks.title: Comparison of Functions #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: and another function is n cube. I want to compare them to know which one is bigger and

chunks.title: Comparison of Functions #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: which one is smaller. So, which becomes an upper bound and which becomes a lower bound.

chunks.title: Comparison of Functions #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So for comparing these two I don't have to check anything. Directly I know that this

chunks.title: Comparison of Functions #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: is smaller and this is greater. But if at all I have to check them, I have to prove,

chunks.title: Comparison of Functions #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: then how I can do? One method is I can sample some values. For example, if I take n as 2,

chunks.title: Comparison of Functions #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: then this is 2 square, right? So it is 4 and this is 2 cube, this is 8. So, 4 is smaller

chunks.title: Comparison of Functions #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: than 8. So, it is greater. If I take it as 3, then 3 square is 9 and this is 3 cube is

chunks.title: Comparison of Functions #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: 27. So, yes, 27 is greater than 9. If I take 4, this is 4 square, it is 16 and this is

chunks.title: Comparison of Functions #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: 4 cube, this is 64. So, I have sampled 3 values. That is enough for me to observe that

chunks.title: Comparison of Functions #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: this is always greater than this one. So, yes, this is smaller than this one. This is

chunks.title: Comparison of Functions #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: one method of checking whether functions are comparable. This is one method of comparing

chunks.title: Comparison of Functions #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: two functions to find out which one is smaller or greater or are they equal. Second method

chunks.title: Comparison of Functions #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: is apply log on both sides. Apply log on both sides. Log n square, log n cube. Then this

chunks.title: Comparison of Functions #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: becomes 2 log n and this becomes 3 log n. Now, this is log n, this is log n. They are

chunks.title: Comparison of Functions #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: same only but this is 2 times, that is 3 times and definitely this is less than that one.

chunks.title: Comparison of Functions #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So second method is apply log on both the sides to find out which one is smaller and

chunks.title: Comparison of Functions #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: which one is greater. So, if you have some complex functions, then this is useful. This

chunks.title: Comparison of Functions #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: is a known function only. So, I am taking a known thing to make you understand what

chunks.title: Comparison of Functions #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: is the method of checking them. So, 2 log n. So, I should check exact values. 2 log

chunks.title: Comparison of Functions #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: n is less than 3 log n. So, for applying logs, you should know some formulas for log. So,

chunks.title: Comparison of Functions #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: let me write some formulas here. Log A into B is log A plus log B. Log A divided by B

chunks.title: Comparison of Functions #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: is log A minus log B. Log A to the power of B is B log A. If you have any power here,

chunks.title: Comparison of Functions #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: that will come this side. It will become coefficient. A power log B base C is B power

chunks.title: Comparison of Functions #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: log A base C. So, this log value can come as a base here if it is in power. And A power

chunks.title: Comparison of Functions #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: B is equals to n. Then B is equals to log n base A. These formulas must be helpful for

chunks.title: Comparison of Functions #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: solving log problems. So, now the objective is to compare functions. So, I will write

chunks.title: Comparison of Functions #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: few functions and let us compare them and find out which one is greater and which one

chunks.title: Comparison of Functions #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: is smaller. Here I have two functions. Let us find out which is greater. Compare them.

chunks.title: Comparison of Functions #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So f of n is n square log n. g of n is n log n whole to the power of 10. So, power of 10

chunks.title: Comparison of Functions #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: is greater. So, it looks as if that is greater than this one. We know n square is greater.

chunks.title: Comparison of Functions #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So now it will confuse. So, if you are unable to judge which one is greater, right? If you

chunks.title: Comparison of Functions #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: are able to judge, then no need to do anything. If you are able to judge it perfectly, then

chunks.title: Comparison of Functions #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: let us compare and find out by applying log. Apply log. So, this is log n square log n.

chunks.title: Comparison of Functions #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: And this is log n log n. This is power 10. If you open this one, this is log n square

chunks.title: Comparison of Functions #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: plus log of log n. As we know log a into b is log a plus log b. So, log a plus log

chunks.title: Comparison of Functions #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: b. Now, this side it will be log n plus log of log n whole to the power of 10. We have

chunks.title: Comparison of Functions #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: used first formula. Now, using the third formula there, this becomes 2 log n plus log

chunks.title: Comparison of Functions #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: of log n. And this is log n plus 10 is in power. So, 10 log of log n. Now, check this.

chunks.title: Comparison of Functions #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: This is the bigger term. This is the bigger term on that side. Now, if you check two bigger

chunks.title: Comparison of Functions #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: terms, this is 2 log n and that is just log n. So, definitely 2 log n is greater than

chunks.title: Comparison of Functions #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: this one. Now, that is 10 log of log n and this is just log of log n. So, that is a smaller

chunks.title: Comparison of Functions #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: value. Log of log n is a smaller value. We can take it as 2 rupees some paisa and 1 rupee

chunks.title: Comparison of Functions #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: some paisa. So, paisa is smaller than rupees. So, this is greater. So, this function is

chunks.title: Comparison of Functions #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: greater than that one. So, this is greater than that one.

chunks.title: Comparison of Functions #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Second example. f of n is 3 n power root n and g of n is 2 power root n log n. Let us compare.

chunks.title: Comparison of Functions #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: So, for comparing, I will first write it as it is. 3 n power root n and this is 2 and this is

chunks.title: Comparison of Functions #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: log n root n log n. So, if you apply this third formula, then this can also be written as n power

chunks.title: Comparison of Functions #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: root n and this is base 2 only. Log base 2. Here is base 2. It is always by default, always it is

chunks.title: Comparison of Functions #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: base 2. So, it is 2 power log n power n. Now, by using the fourth formula, what I can do is,

chunks.title: Comparison of Functions #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: this I can interchange with this one. So, this will be 3 n power root n and that n power root

chunks.title: Comparison of Functions #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: n comes here and whole to the power of log, this 2 goes there. So, 2 base 2.

chunks.title: Comparison of Functions #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Log 2 base 2 is 1. So, it is power 1. So, what is the answer? 3 n root n and this is n power root n.

chunks.title: Comparison of Functions #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: Now, I have two functions. So, I did not apply log without applying log. I have not applied log

chunks.title: Comparison of Functions #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: on both the sides but I have used the formulas of log for solving them. Even if you apply log,

chunks.title: Comparison of Functions #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: also you can get something. So, now what do you see? 3 n power root n and n power root n. So,

chunks.title: Comparison of Functions #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: if you see the term, they are same. This is just a coefficient. It is just like 2 n square and n

chunks.title: Comparison of Functions #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: square. Who is greater? They are equal. This coefficient is ignorable. Asymptotic

chunks.title: Comparison of Functions #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: they are equal. Value wise, this is greater. Order of n if you write, big O of n square if you write,

chunks.title: Comparison of Functions #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: big O of n square, big O of n square. Asymptotically, they are equal but the value

chunks.title: Comparison of Functions #1 - Abdul Bari
chunks.text: wise this is greater than that one. So, if you want asymptotic answer, then both are equal.

text: Now, a combination of two functions. The functions may be time functions or they may be space functions. So, functions, mathematical functions. If suppose I have one function that is n square and another function is n cube. I want to compare them to know which one is bigger and which one is smaller. So, which becomes an upper bound and which becomes a lower bound. So for comparing these two I don't have to check anything. Directly I know that this is smaller and this is greater. But if at all I have to check them, I have to prove, then how I can do? One method is I can sample some values. For example, if I take n as 2, then this is 2 square, right? So it is 4 and this is 2 cube, this is 8. So, 4 is smaller than 8. So, it is greater. If I take it as 3, then 3 square is 9 and this is 3 cube is 27. So, yes, 27 is greater than 9. If I take 4, this is 4 square, it is 16 and this is 4 cube, this is 64. So, I have sampled 3 values. That is enough for me to observe that this is always greater than this one. So, yes, this is smaller than this one. This is one method of checking whether functions are comparable. This is one method of comparing two functions to find out which one is smaller or greater or are they equal. Second method is apply log on both sides. Apply log on both sides. Log n square, log n cube. Then this becomes 2 log n and this becomes 3 log n. Now, this is log n, this is log n. They are same only but this is 2 times, that is 3 times and definitely this is less than that one. So second method is apply log on both the sides to find out which one is smaller and which one is greater. So, if you have some complex functions, then this is useful. This is a known function only. So, I am taking a known thing to make you understand what is the method of checking them. So, 2 log n. So, I should check exact values. 2 log n is less than 3 log n. So, for applying logs, you should know some formulas for log. So, let me write some formulas here. Log A into B is log A plus log B. Log A divided by B is log A minus log B. Log A to the power of B is B log A. If you have any power here, that will come this side. It will become coefficient. A power log B base C is B power log A base C. So, this log value can come as a base here if it is in power. And A power B is equals to n. Then B is equals to log n base A. These formulas must be helpful for solving log problems. So, now the objective is to compare functions. So, I will write few functions and let us compare them and find out which one is greater and which one is smaller. Here I have two functions. Let us find out which is greater. Compare them. So f of n is n square log n. g of n is n log n whole to the power of 10. So, power of 10 is greater. So, it looks as if that is greater than this one. We know n square is greater. So now it will confuse. So, if you are unable to judge which one is greater, right? If you are able to judge, then no need to do anything. If you are able to judge it perfectly, then let us compare and find out by applying log. Apply log. So, this is log n square log n. And this is log n log n. This is power 10. If you open this one, this is log n square plus log of log n. As we know log a into b is log a plus log b. So, log a plus log b. Now, this side it will be log n plus log of log n whole to the power of 10. We have used first formula. Now, using the third formula there, this becomes 2 log n plus log of log n. And this is log n plus 10 is in power. So, 10 log of log n. Now, check this. This is the bigger term. This is the bigger term on that side. Now, if you check two bigger terms, this is 2 log n and that is just log n. So, definitely 2 log n is greater than this one. Now, that is 10 log of log n and this is just log of log n. So, that is a smaller value. Log of log n is a smaller value. We can take it as 2 rupees some paisa and 1 rupee some paisa. So, paisa is smaller than rupees. So, this is greater. So, this function is greater than that one. So, this is greater than that one. Second example. f of n is 3 n power root n and g of n is 2 power root n log n. Let us compare. So, for comparing, I will first write it as it is. 3 n power root n and this is 2 and this is log n root n log n. So, if you apply this third formula, then this can also be written as n power root n and this is base 2 only. Log base 2. Here is base 2. It is always by default, always it is base 2. So, it is 2 power log n power n. Now, by using the fourth formula, what I can do is, this I can interchange with this one. So, this will be 3 n power root n and that n power root n comes here and whole to the power of log, this 2 goes there. So, 2 base 2. Log 2 base 2 is 1. So, it is power 1. So, what is the answer? 3 n root n and this is n power root n. Now, I have two functions. So, I did not apply log without applying log. I have not applied log on both the sides but I have used the formulas of log for solving them. Even if you apply log, also you can get something. So, now what do you see? 3 n power root n and n power root n. So, if you see the term, they are same. This is just a coefficient. It is just like 2 n square and n square. Who is greater? They are equal. This coefficient is ignorable. Asymptotic they are equal. Value wise, this is greater. Order of n if you write, big O of n square if you write, big O of n square, big O of n square. Asymptotically, they are equal but the value wise this is greater than that one. So, if you want asymptotic answer, then both are equal.
